2603.25268.title: CRAFT: Grounded Multi-Agent Coordination Under Partial Information
2603.25268.paper_summary: We introduce CRAFT, a multi-agent benchmark for evaluating pragmatic communication in large language models under strict partial information. In this setting, multiple agents with complementary but incomplete views must coordinate through natural language to construct a shared 3D structure that no single agent can fully observe. We formalize this problem as a multi-sender pragmatic reasoning task and provide a diagnostic framework that decomposes failures into spatial grounding, belief modeling and pragmatic communication errors, including a taxonomy of behavioral failure profiles in both frontier and open-weight models. Across a diverse set of models, including 8 open-weight and 7 frontier including reasoning models, we find that stronger reasoning ability does not reliably translate to better coordination: smaller open-weight models often match or outperform frontier systems, and improved individual communication does not guarantee successful collaboration. These results suggest that multi-agent coordination remains a fundamentally unsolved challenge for current language models. Our code can be found at https://github.com/csu-signal/CRAFT
2603.25268.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹CRAFT，一個用於評估大型語言模型在嚴格部分信息下進行務實溝通的多代理基準。在這個設定中，具有互補但不完整視角的多個代理必須通過自然語言協調，以構建一個單一代理無法完全觀察的共享3D結構。我們將這個問題形式化為一個多發送者的務實推理任務，並提供一個診斷框架，將失敗分解為空間基礎、信念建模和務實溝通錯誤，包括邊界和開放權重模型中的行為失敗類型。我們在一組多樣的模型中進行了研究，包括8個開放權重和7個邊界模型，包括推理模型，我們發現更強的推理能力並不可靠地轉化為更好的協調：較小的開放權重模型往往與邊界系統相匹配或超越，而改善個體溝通並不保證成功的協作。這些結果表明，多代理協調仍然是當前語言模型面臨的一個根本未解挑戰。我們的代碼可以在https://github.com/csu-signal/CRAFT找到。
2603.25268.author: Abhijnan Nath et.al.
2603.25268.authors: Abhijnan Nath, Hannah VanderHoeven, Nikhil Krishnaswamy
